__doc:
Locked perf baseline enforced by scripts/test/perf-gate.sh. Hardware fingerprint mismatch auto-downgrades to WARN (not FAIL). Regenerate with 'bash scripts/test/perf-gate.sh --run-id manual --capture-baselines' then commit this file alongside the change.

SCHEMA: each metric is either (a) a bare number — gated against tolerance_pct, or (b) a dict {method: sigma, central, stdev, k_sigma, n_samples, ...} — gated as central ± k_sigma·stdev. The dict form lets tolerance bands track measurement noise: as the underlying benchmark gets less noisy stdev shrinks and the band tightens automatically — a fixed percentage threshold cannot do that. Migration to per-metric σ-bounds is tracked by issue #738; this file holds whichever form is currently calibrated for each metric.

burst_registration_ops_sec.note: Replaces the global 10% tolerance for this metric. Natural CV of ~9.20% sat at the boundary of the percentage threshold and produced false-positive regressions on ~25% of runs (#530, #738). 2σ bound is principled (mean ± 2 sample-stdev). Re-captured at v0.12.0 release prep against N≥300 will sharpen σ; the band will tighten automatically without a config change.
